should_trigger: Design the monitoring and alerting model for our agent orchestrator so on-call only gets paged for real user-impacting failures.
Review these orchestrator metrics and tell me whether they cover latency, traffic, errors, saturation, and replay/debug needs.
We have too many noisy alerts from tool retries and dependency errors. Redesign the alert policy and dashboards for the auto-orchestrator.
What should we log versus expose as metrics for model calls, tool calls, queue depth, policy denials, and run replays?
should_not_trigger: Summarize the Google SRE book chapter on monitoring in a few paragraphs for a reading group.
Choose between Datadog, Grafana Cloud, and New Relic pricing for our startup.
Write a generic OpenTelemetry instrumentation tutorial for a Flask app with no orchestrator or alerting design context.
edge_cases: Our orchestrator has distributed traces but no SLO dashboards. Decide whether this is an observability design problem or just a tracing implementation task, and apply the skill only if alerting and operational coverage are in scope.
We need audit logs for compliance. Use this skill only if the logs also need to support reliability diagnosis, alerting, incident triage, or replay.
